notes: Maps to Berlin's 'Grand Jury Prize' / second-tier prize. Modern wikilink target [[Silver Bear Grand Jury Prize]]; older years used [[Jury Grand Prix|Silver Bear Special Jury Prize]] (cer 2000-2009ish). Distinct from the Berlin 'Silver Bear Jury Prize' (no 'Grand') which is a SEPARATE third prize introduced more recently — that prize is OUT OF SCOPE for the 11 user categories and is filtered out by exact target matching.

Gendered Best Actor prize active 2000-2020 only. Berlin consolidated acting prizes into gender-neutral Best Leading Performance + Best Supporting Performance starting at the 71st Berlinale (cer 2021).
Gendered Best Actress prize active 2000-2020 only. See berlin.silver_bear_best_actor note.

display_name: Silver Bear for Best Leading Performance
wikilink_targets: Silver Bear for Best Leading Performance
display_aliases: Silver Bear for Best Leading Performance
notes: Gender-neutral acting prize introduced at the 71st Berlinale (cer 2021), replacing the gendered Best Actor + Best Actress.

display_name: Silver Bear for Best Supporting Performance
wikilink_targets: Silver Bear for Best Supporting Performance

notes: Gender-neutral acting prize introduced at the 71st Berlinale (cer 2021), replacing the gendered Best Actor + Best Actress.

display_name: Silver Bear for Outstanding Artistic Contribution

display_aliases: Silver Bear for Outstanding Artistic Contribution
Silver Bear for an Outstanding Artistic Contribution
Silver Bear for Outstanding Artistic Achievement
notes: Berlin's craft/technical prize. Display name varies year-to-year: sometimes plain 'Outstanding Artistic Contribution', sometimes with parenthetical specialty (Camera/cinematography/editing/music/costume etc.). Pre-2024 articles use bold-only display ('''Silver Bear...''') with no wikilink; cer 2024+ uses wikilinked target. Both display-alias and wikilink-target matching needed.

Innovation/'opens new perspectives in cinema' prize named after Berlinale founder Alfred Bauer. RETIRED in 2020 after revelations about Bauer's Nazi-era ties. Active 2000-2019 only. Phase D era_override gap suppresses MISSING REVIEW for cer 2020-2025.

Berlin's debut-feature prize, equivalent to Cannes Caméra d'Or / Venice Lion of the Future. Introduced at the 56th Berlinale (cer 2006) — does NOT exist for 2000-2005. Typically appears in a SEPARATE === Best First Feature Award === or === GWFF Best First Feature Award === subsection within Official Awards (renamed to GWFF after sponsorship deal); section-heading-driven Pass 2 catches it. Phase D era_override gap suppresses MISSING REVIEW for cer 2000-2005.
